behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic rule evaluation (threshold lookup + score math) inside the scoring model, with no required OS/network/subprocess and no AI output dependence. Higher levels were considered and rejected: the behavior text is about 'assigns the correct risk score' for a rule condition, not cross-service contracts.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the scoring function with merchant-category configuration and a transaction amount just above threshold; assert exact numeric score contribution and final score.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real AI/ML model judgment ('identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern'). Stubbed outputs would hide model-quality failures. System was considered but rejected because no explicit external HTTP/OS contract or round-trip side effect is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on representative transaction-context sequences (including rapid low-amount geo-distributed purchases) and assert fraud probability/label crosses the fraud decision threshold at an acceptable rate.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Likely failure mode is module handoff between transaction-history retrieval/time-window filtering/scoring logic, which unit-only checks can miss. System was considered and rejected: behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess or explicit external contract readback. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because this is non-ML counting logic.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring scoring service with local transaction store/repository and clock inputs; seed per-user transactions across window boundaries, execute velocity check, and assert count used by scoring is correct.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration/decision logic (combining two model outcomes). Agentic was considered and rejected because the behavior precondition fixes model outputs ('flagged by risk model but not by scoring model'), so the test can focus on gating logic with controlled outputs, not model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test the decision pipeline with scoring output=false and risk output=true from model interfaces/stubs; assert transaction state transitions to held-for-review.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: This is output composition/serialization across modules (score + probability + flag provenance). System was considered and rejected because behavior text does not explicitly require validating a real external API/UI contract or round-trip external readback. Agentic rejected since requirement is field presence/structure, not model-quality correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test the aggregator/output mapper with both model results present; assert output payload contains numeric risk score, fraud probability, and per-model flag indicators/reason fields consumed by review flow.
